title: Transactional lane sequence
statement: Implementations shall execute the seam transaction lane as `prepare -> open_session -> capability_view -> execute -> commit` with deterministic phase outcomes.

title: Stable identity and fence tuple
statement: Sessions and commits shall be fenced by `formula_stable_id`, `formula_token`, `snapshot_epoch`, `bind_hash`, and `profile_version`.
notes: The fence tuple must be modelable and replayable.

title: Atomic commit bundle
statement: Accepted commits shall publish one atomic bundle containing `value_delta`, `shape_delta`, `topology_delta`, and optional `format_delta` or `display_delta`.

title: Candidate result distinct from publication
statement: Successful evaluation shall first yield an accepted candidate result distinct from committed publication; evaluator success alone is not publication.
notes: Preserves evaluator output versus coordinator publication.

title: Minimum payload schema sufficiency
statement: Candidate results, commit bundles, spill events, typed reject contexts, and trace payloads shall expose minimum typed field sets sufficient for deterministic coordinator interpretation and replay.
notes: Schema-level tightening without freezing final encodings.

title: Publication-surface delta derivation
statement: When prepared-result hints cross the seam as explicit publication obligations, OxFml shall derive typed `format_delta` or `display_delta` artifacts rather than leaving those consequences implicit in local evaluator state.

title: Topology facts not policy
statement: `topology_delta` shall carry evaluator facts and dependency evidence only; global recalc policy remains OxCalc-owned.

title: Typed dependency consequence facts
statement: `topology_delta` shall surface typed dependency consequence facts where additions, removals, or reclassifications need replay-stable explanation beyond plain dependency identity lists.

title: Session-bound capability authority
statement: Capability decisions shall be session-bound, commit-validated, and machine-typed on denial.

title: Fence incompatibility consequences
statement: Fence or capability incompatibility shall yield typed no-publish rejection rather than partial publication or ambiguous failure.

title: Overlay lifecycle discipline
statement: Dynamic-reference, spill, and format overlays shall be session-local until commit and fenced by epoch, token, bind hash, and profile version.
notes: Designed for replay and formal modeling.

title: Coordinator-relevant runtime effect reporting
statement: Runtime-derived evaluator effects that materially affect coordinator correctness shall be surfaced directly or be derivable from typed candidate-result payloads and events.
notes: Covers dynamic refs, spill, format dependency, and related surfaced facts.

title: Typed delta and effect taxonomy
statement: Published deltas and surfaced evaluator effects shall use explicit typed family taxonomies rather than opaque generic buckets.

title: Execution restriction surface
statement: Scheduler-relevant execution restrictions shall be surfaced as typed formula-level facts including thread-affine, host-query, async-coupled, serial-only, and single-flight-advisable lanes without leaking scheduler policy into OxFml.

title: Runtime async overlay preservation
statement: Async-coupled runtime lanes may surface typed overlay or effect material for replay and scheduler consumption, but those surfaced runtime facts remain evaluator truth rather than coordinator policy.

title: Spill event typing
statement: Spill transitions shall be explicit typed events (`SpillTakeover`,`SpillClearance`,`SpillBlocked`) within seam outputs.

title: Prepared semantic boundary
statement: The evaluator seam shall preserve the prepared semantic distinctions needed by OxFunc, including direct-scalar vs array-like vs reference-visible inputs and typed result classes.
notes: Depends on the OxFml formula-engine architecture contract.

title: Caller-context preservation
statement: Caller-context-dependent scalarization and reference formation shall remain explicit across the OxFml to OxFunc boundary.

title: Locale and format service boundary
statement: Locale identity, workbook date system, and format-language services required by function semantics shall be exposed as explicit evaluator facilities rather than hidden host fallbacks.

title: Typed host-query capability boundary
statement: Host-observing function lanes such as `CELL` and `INFO` shall consume typed capability-scoped host-query views rather than raw host objects or ad hoc callbacks.
notes: Preserves OxFunc/OxFml boundary discipline for host facts.

title: Helper-environment semantic-plan profile
statement: The OxFml semantic plan shall surface helper-environment profile facts for `LET`, `LAMBDA`, helper invocation, and lexical helper capture need when those distinctions affect replay or downstream semantic coordination.

title: Typed reject outcomes
statement: Reject outcomes shall be machine-typed and replay-stable via `reject_code` plus typed context.
notes: Opaque reject strings are not contractual.

title: Trace schema contract
statement: When tracing is enabled, seam events shall carry a versioned schema identifier and schema-validatable structured fields.
notes: Trace schema is part of the conformance surface.

title: Trace event taxonomy
statement: Trace output shall distinguish lifecycle, candidate-result, commit, reject, and surfaced-effect event families sufficiently for deterministic replay.

title: Formal contract coupling
statement: Each important seam contract clause shall map to a replay artifact, a conformance pack, or a formal model obligation in Lean or TLA+.
notes: Current local checked artifacts now include `formal/lean/OxFmlSessionLifecycle.lean`, `formal/lean/OxFmlExternalReferenceDeferred.lean`, `formal/tla/FecSessionLifecycle.tla`, `formal/tla/FecExternalCapabilityGate.tla`, and `formal/run_formal.ps1`.

title: DNA OneCalc compatibility mode
statement: A reduced-profile single-node host may exercise the same evaluator contracts without implying OxCalc-owned scheduler policy.

title: Replay adapter projection preserves OxFml semantics
statement: Replay normalization for OxFml shall preserve source schema ids, typed seam artifacts, candidate-versus-publication separation, and typed reject/effect semantics without flattening local meaning into generic replay prose.

title: Witness lifecycle and reduced-witness governance
statement: Reduced OxFml replay witnesses shall carry explicit lifecycle state, retained-versus-pack eligibility boundaries, and reduction-manifest provenance; explanatory-only and quarantined outputs shall remain non-pack-eligible.

title: Local normalized pack-candidate bundle evidence
statement: Local normalized replay bundle evidence shall preserve source schema ids, witness refs, and explicit non-pack-eligible state while broader pack-grade promotion remains out of scope.

title: Minimum library-context snapshot floor
statement: Where formula admission, replay classification, or provider-stage explanation depends on external catalog truth, OxFml semantic plans shall preserve a minimum per-surface library-context floor including stable surface identity, name-resolution table reference, semantic trait/profile reference, and gating/profile reference.

title: Typed callable minimum carrier
statement: When a prepared result is semantically callable, OxFml shall preserve a typed minimum callable carrier including origin kind, invocation model, capture mode, and arity in addition to replay-oriented callable summaries.

title: Session timeout and cleanup
statement: Session expiry, abort, and overlay-cleanup semantics require explicit reject and cleanup rules before promotion.

title: Coordinator contention replay
statement: Deterministic contention replay for interleaved commits is required before multi-session promotion.

title: Parallel coordinator hardening
statement: Thread-safe coordinator/session storage and deterministic conflict handling are required before async/MVCC seam claims.

title: Concurrent TLA+ model
statement: A TLA+ model for concurrent evaluator sessions, retries, and publish fences is required before Stage 2 promotion.
notes: Concurrency must be model-checked, not only tested.
